cards.back: x(t) = u(t) − u(t−T). On from 0 to T, zero elsewhere. X(s) = (1−e^(−sT))/s.
ℒ{f(t−T)u(t−T)} = e^(−sT)F(s). Delay by T seconds → multiply by e^(−sT) in s-domain.
y_pulse(t) = y_step(t) − y_step(t−T). Superposition: subtract delayed copy.
Y(s) = H(s)·X(s). Find X(s), multiply, PFE, inverse ℒ. Works for ANY input.
Only first step is active: y = 1−e^(−t/RC). Capacitor charging. Same as step response.
Both steps active: y = (1−e^(−t/RC))−(1−e^(−(t−1)/RC)) = e^(−(t−1)/RC)−e^(−t/RC). Discharge.
y(∞) = 0. After finite-duration input, stable LTI system returns to rest. Capacitor fully discharges.
Sum of shifted steps: u(t)+u(t−T)+u(t−2T)+... Output = sum of shifted step responses.
From time-shift: shifting f(t) right by T in time = multiplying F(s) by e^(−sT) in s. Phase shift e^(−jωT) at each frequency.

cards.front: Can this method handle ANY input?
cards.back: Yes! Any input → X(s) → Y(s)=H(s)X(s) → PFE → y(t). Or decompose into steps/impulses and use superposition.
Compute one basic response (step/impulse). Reuse for all composed inputs via shift/scale/sum. No need to redo from scratch.

cards.front: Pulse input x(t)=u(t)−u(t−2) through H=1/(1+s). Find Y(s).
cards.back: Y(s) = (1−e^(−2s))/[s(1+s)]. Split: 1/[s(1+s)] − e^(−2s)/[s(1+s)]. PFE first term, then shift second by 2.
